meaning: n.(浓烈或难闻的)烟,气体,一阵愤怒(或不安)v.用烟熏,冒烟,发怒
examples: As I said, it's a particularly hazardous substance and is worked with in fume hoods.
Lie precariously at the edge of the mattress and fume if you must, but lie in the same bed.
It has slashed the cost of toll roads to almost one-tenth of the normal level, attracting yet more people on to fume-choked roads.
The ACLU may fume, but the authors of the Bill of Rights can rest in peace.
The victims were found in a fume-filled garage in Castlerock, Co Londonderry, in May 1991.
